definition: AI capital expenditure can be diagnosed via Minsky financing classification: hyperscaler capex (MSFT, GOOG, AMZN, META) funded from cash flows constitutes hedge finance, while downstream ecosystem (AI startups, data center REITs, GPU leasing) increasingly relies on venture debt and leveraged structures constituting speculative or Ponzi finance.
evidence: Hyperscaler capex primarily funded from cash flows — hedge finance
Downstream ecosystem increasingly reliant on venture debt and leveraged structures
Monitoring variable: debt service ratios of AI-adjacent companies
pair_2 called it a genuinely useful analytical framework
open_questions: What is the aggregate size of downstream speculative/Ponzi AI financing?
At what point does downstream distress feed back to hyperscaler investment decisions?
Can this framework produce actionable early warning signals?
